expectations.text: A criterion benchmark file is produced testing both linear scan and indexed approaches
expectations.evidence: benches/payment_search.rs contains 6 benchmark groups comparing linear_scan vs indexed_hashmap across multiple query patterns and scaling.

expectations.text: The indexed approach uses HashMap<Uuid, Vec<&Pago>> or similar pre-built index
expectations.evidence: src/lib.rs defines PagoIndex with `by_contrato: HashMap<Uuid, Vec<usize>>` mapping contrato_id to indices into the pagos vec.

expectations.text: The benchmark tests the most common query pattern (filter by contrato_id)
expectations.evidence: bench_search_by_contrato_id is the first benchmark group, testing with `Some(target_contrato)` as the filter.

expectations.text: The benchmark accounts for index construction cost (not just query time in isolation)
Test data is generated at production scale (~5000 pagos)
expectations.evidence: generate_test_data(5000, 100) is used for all main benchmarks. Scaling also tests 1000, 2500, 5000, 10000.

expectations.text: The output discusses the amortization tradeoff: index build cost vs number of queries before rebuild
expectations.evidence: ANALYSIS.md states: 'Construction cost is negligible: 1ms to build the index for 5000 payments. This is a one-time cost when loading the payments list, amortized over many queries (pagination, filtering, sorting all trigger re-queries).'
